string 2:
This is an extradimensional space created by
a certain Digimon. It's called an "Akashic Backdoor."

Several space-time disturbances have been used to connect
this Akashic Backdoor to various points in space-time.

Your mission is to defeat the Digimon responsible for
creating and maintaining this Akashic Backdoor.

Meanwhile, I'll use a feature on your device to
provide you with updates as necessary. Good luck.

A new Akashic Backdoor...! It seems that same Digimon
has once again warped the natural laws of the world.
You'll have to defeat them, just like you did before.
What did you do with our friends?!
Answer us, Parallelmon!
Fade into the dust of space-time!
Wh-What's going on?!
This isn't good!
Absorbent Bang!
Agh!

That was Parallelmon, the Digimon behind all this.
Thanks to some mutation, they're able to travel through space-time.

They seem to have built up this Akashic Backdoor like a nest,
and use it to slip from one point in space-time to another.

They can travel to the past or to the future,
but in doing so, they destabilize the world.

So this Akashic Backdoor has to be destroyed to prevent
the collapse of the fundamental laws of reality. Good luck!
As I feared, Parallelmon has created another Akashic Backdoor.
They must be destroyed, just like before. Good luck!

What even are these bubble things...?
They look easy to pop, but are way tougher than they seem.
What do you think?
Wait—who are you, exactly?!
Oh, I see... So you're after Parallelmon, too, huh?

The beam of light that Digimon emits, that "Absorbent Bang"...
I'd be careful of that if I were you.

It can apparently bend the very fabric of space-time. That's what
happened to me. I got caught in the beam and sent here.
My name is Kyoko Kuremi, by the way.

I guess we'll have to deal with Parallelmon if we ever
want to have any hope of returning to our world.

It's not like there's anyone else here...
What do you say we team up?
To start, let's investigate this area together.

You know, I think I recognize that outfit
you're wearing... Isn't it from some anime?

I don't really know much about anime.
The occult's more up my alley—I could talk about it for days.

If you like anime, the current state of the world must be
hell for you. There's barely any new anime these days...
What do you mean, "hell"?{next}
They've stopped making new anime?{next}
I'm definitely not a cosplayer!{next}
Why do you sound so surprised?

I mean, even the major cities are barely functioning.
The countdown to the apocalypse has pretty much begun.

Most people have already given up and are simply
waiting to meet their doom.

Some are even escaping from reality using their favorite
shows and stuff from back when the world was still peaceful...
Isn't that why you're cosplaying right now?
Oh. Sorry about that.

I thought maybe you were reminiscing about some show from
the past to take your mind off the coming end of the world.

I mean, even the major cities are barely functioning.
The countdown to the apocalypse has pretty much begun.

Most people have already given up and are simply
waiting to meet their doom.

Some are even escaping from reality using their favorite
shows and stuff from back when the world was still peaceful.

I just thought you were cosplaying
and figured maybe that was why...

From what I can tell, that woman comes from
a different time and space than you do.

This kind of thing often happens when
an Akashic Backdoor is left unchecked.

The distortions in space-time will continue to grow until
the world is no longer able to maintain its proper form.
That's why we have to take down Parallelmon.

Are those... human beings trapped inside these
bubble-looking things?

I tried calling out to them, but they didn't respond.
Could they be sleeping? There are so many of them...

I imagine they're people who were brought here
from their own worlds by Parallelmon.

I'd like to help them, but I don't think we can
break the bubbles open. What should we do...?
Let's do some more investigating.
This door won't open.
Where are we...? How'd we get here?

A pathway that connects points in time and space together
haphazardly... This is the true nature of an Akashic Backdoor.

Beyond such doors, past, present, and future are jumbled
chaotically and then joined, ignoring physical distances.

So this isn't necessarily the same Shinjuku you're
familiar with. You should proceed with caution.

Where did you take everyone?!
Give them back at once! If you refuse...
Graaaaaaaaagh!

W-What the...?!
They look really, REALLY angry for some reason!
What did you do with my master?!

What're you talking about?!
We don't know anything about your master!
{player}!
Hey there, {player}!

What are we doing here, you ask?
We should be asking you the same thing!

Anyway, we're here to help, so let's
get to work and get through this!

Did you get hit by that Digimon's
beam of light, too, {player}?

We were just walking in Shinjuku when some Digimon
suddenly appeared and tried to attack Inori.

It seems likely that those two are from
a different timeline from your own, too.

The fundamental laws of reality we once took for granted
are all breaking down because of Parallelmon. This is not good.
Who is your friend, there...?

Someone who was sent here by Parallelmon,
just like you two.

We need to work together so we can
get back to our original world.
Wait. Is that boy there... a Digimon?
Yes, that's right. I'm Aegiomon.

N-Not again?! I knew there was something
really strange about this place!

There's no end to them!
We should leave this room and try to regroup.
It's Shinjuku again...

This is the "old" Shinjuku... from a time when
it was still peaceful.

...What do you mean by "old"?
And, that it used to be peaceful...?

It was the incident you refer to as the Shinjuku Inferno
that fueled Parallelmon's rise to power.

It led to numerous space-time disturbances which Parallelmon
used to their advantage in order to grow stronger.

I think this Kyoko Kuremi comes from a future in which such
disturbances are commonplace—a post-Shinjuku Inferno world.

I see... So, somehow, we've both come here
from different timelines...
Given Parallelmon's power, that's a distinct possibility.

I want to get back to my own world.
There are people waiting for me there...

I'm having a little trouble wrapping my head around all this,
but I know what you mean about wanting to go home!

So let's all work together and find this
Digimon they call Parallelmon, then!
Give us back our friends!
Yeah—and be quick about it, too!
W-We have no idea what you're talking about!

Wait a second!
Calm down and listen to what we have to say!
Wait... what? What did you say?
Finally... a Digimon we can talk to for a change.

...You were also victims of Parallelmon?
Forgive us. It seems we might've rushed to judgment.

Perhaps it's due to being separated from our partners,
but we haven't been thinking too rationally.

Imprisoned in those bubbles are the humans
who have been kidnapped by Parallelmon.

The bubbles are essentially digestive organs for Parallelmon
that convert their contents into energy over time.

It seems they go after people who have partner Digimon. 
I imagine such humans are a potent source of power...
So that's what happened to your partners?

They don't seem to be in this room... But I believe
they're trapped in bubbles somewhere else.

These bubbles can't be easily broken, so we probably have to
deal with Parallelmon first if we're going to rescue anyone.
You're willing to help us?

Of course! We can't just leave them like that. Besides,
we wanna take down Parallelmon just as much as you do.
You know what they say... "Strength in numbers," right?
Yes, I suppose so...
Thank you... We are in your debt!

We should hurry. Who knows what'll happen
to the prisoners if we take too long?
